index.name: Bharat 22 Index
index.description: Bharat 22 ETF is the Government of India's own investment product — born from the NDA government's innovative approach to divestment. Instead of selling PSU stakes to foreign institutions at discounts, the government listed its strategic holdings as an ETF, enabling ordinary Indian citizens to co-own the nation's crown jewel companies alongside the government. The 22-stock Bharat 22 Index includes companies where government holds direct equity or through SUUTI (Specified Undertaking of Unit Trust of India — the entity that took over UTI's strategic stakes in ITC, L&T and Axis Bank). This makes Bharat 22 uniquely India's only index where you hold the same companies as the Union Government of India. The composition is deliberately diverse: private-sector titans (ITC, L&T, Axis Bank), PSU banks (SBI, Bank of Baroda), energy conglomerates (ONGC, IOC, BPCL, GAIL), power infrastructure (Power Grid, NTPC), basic industries (BHEL, SAIL, Coal India), and specialty PSUs (NBCC, NLC India, Engineers India). This diversity mirrors India's mixed economy — private enterprise and government-directed industrialisation side by side. ICICIB22 has seen multiple government-mandated FFOs (Further Fund Offers) where the government sold its stake at discounted NAV to retail investors — creating periodic buying opportunities. Bharat 22 has delivered strong returns during periods of PSU/value rallies (FY20-FY22: 120%+ returns). Concentrated risk: any government policy change affecting PSU sectors directly impacts this ETF. With ₹20,000+ Cr AUM and strong government backing, ICICIB22 is among the most unique ETF products globally — a government directly inviting citizens to share in the nation's economic enterprises.

holdings.name: ITC Ltd
holdings.description: Bharat 22's largest holding — SUUTI holds ~8% of ITC representing the UTI legacy stake. ITC's unique position: cigarettes fund one of India's fastest-growing FMCG empires (Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Bingo). Hotels demerger in progress. 87% dividend payout ratio makes ITC a consistent income stock. ESG headwind from tobacco mitigated by diversification into agribusiness, paperboards and branded foods.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: SUUTI holds L&T stake in Bharat 22. L&T is India's engineering backbone — Rs 5 lakh crore+ order book spanning roads, bridges, metros, nuclear plants, defence systems, data centres and shipbuilding. L&T's presence in Bharat 22 alongside PSUs illustrates how India's infrastructure growth depends on both government investment and private execution.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: SUUTI holds ~8.2% stake in Axis Bank — a legacy of UTI's original shareholding. Axis Bank is one of India's fastest-growing private banks post-Citibank acquisition, expanding its premium credit card and wealth management franchise. Being in Bharat 22 alongside government PSUs while itself being a privately-run bank illustrates the ETF's unique hybrid composition.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest public sector bank with Rs 59 lakh crore balance sheet. Government holds 57.5% stake — SBI is systemically important to India's financial system. Finances 40%+ of India's agriculture, infrastructure and MSME credit. SBI's subsidiaries (SBI Life, SBI Cards, SBI Mutual Fund) are among India's most valuable financial services franchises.

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: India's premier oil and gas explorer — government holds 58% stake. Domestic crude production powers India's energy security. KG Basin deepwater project adding new reserves. ONGC Videsh operates in 15 countries. Perpetually undervalued due to government-controlled pricing — but dividend yield 4-5%+ provides steady return regardless of capital gains.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corporation
holdings.description: India's transmission monopoly — government holds 51%+ stake. 1.75 lakh+ circuit km of inter-state high voltage lines. Regulated 15.5% ROE business with near-zero earnings risk. Renewable energy corridors (HVDC lines from Ladakh, Rajasthan solar farms) are next decade's growth driver. Highest credit-rated PSU after NTPC.

holdings.name: NTPC Ltd
holdings.description: India's largest power generator with 73GW+ installed capacity. Government holds 51.1%. NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd targeting 60GW by 2032 — the biggest green energy transition by any Indian PSU. Regulated return model guarantees earnings stability. 45%+ dividend payout means attractive yield during market corrections.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil refining and distribution company — government holds 52%. 80+ million metric tonnes refining capacity across 9 refineries. 29,000+ petrol stations covering rural India. Petrochemical expansions at Panipat and Paradip refineries improving margins. Dividend yield 5-7%+ in normalised marketing margin years.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal producer — government holds 63%. 700+ million tonnes annual production. India's baseload power depends on Coal India for 50%+ of electricity generation. Dividend yield 5%+ consistently mandated by government. Volume ramp to 1BT per annum target by FY27 could significantly expand earnings. Transition risk real but 15-year timeline.

holdings.name: BPCL (Bharat Petroleum)
holdings.description: Once scheduled for full privatisation (2020 plan abandoned), BPCL remains government-owned with 53% stake. Kochi Super Refinery (Integrated Refinery Expansion) adding complexity. City Gas distribution through wholly-owned Bharat Gas. EV charging network being expanded. Privatisation narrative can re-emerge any election cycle — creating re-rating option.
